title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
Mac2 is characterized by the expression of F13A1 and
transcription factors associated with alternative activation and suppression of immune
responses (NR4A1, NR4A2, KLF4) and notably, is more closely aligned
with fetal macrophages
We observed an increase in Mac2 in AD and psoriasis skin

Adult healthy skin
Mac2 aligned with fetal skin, gut, kidney, liver and thymus macrophages

This
led us to hypothesize a shared cellular program between fetal macrophages and Mac2
in AD and psoriasis skin
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) define Mac2 (Macro_2) as an F13A1+ alternatively activated macrophage with NR4A1/NR4A2/KLF4 transcription factors that aligns transcriptionally with fetal macrophages and increases in AD and psoriasis.

title: Antigen-Presenting Cells in Psoriasis
quotes: Mac2, on the other hand, displays expression of FXIIIA and markers of alternative activation and immune suppression.
Mac2 macrophages' transcriptomic profile closely aligns with that of foetal macrophages in embryonic skin
summary: Antal et al. (2022) confirm Mac2/Macro_2 identity: FXIIIA (F13A1) expression with alternative activation markers, and transcriptomic alignment with fetal macrophages.

title: Macrophages in Skin Wounds: Functions and Therapeutic Potential
quotes: Another population of Mac2 + macrophages displaying an alternative activation state with upregulated NR4A1, NR4A2 and KLF4 has also been reported in human skin and can be identified by F13A1
Mac2 macrophages' transcriptomic profile closely aligns with that of foetal macrophages in embryonic skin
summary: Sim et al. (2022) independently confirm Mac2/Macro_2 as an NR4A1/NR4A2/KLF4+ F13A1+ alternatively activated macrophage state that aligns with fetal skin macrophages.
